solution.p: We will factor 64r^{2}-48rx+9x^{2} where a=64, b=-48, and c=9.

Perfect Square Formula: The formula for perfect square trinomials is (a \pm b)^2=a^2 \pm 2ab + b^2. Note that the polynomial 64r^{2}-48rx+9x^{2} matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]64r^{2}-48rx+9x^{2}\amp =\left(8r\right)^2-2\left(8r\right)\left(3x\right)+\left(3x\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(8r-3x\right)\mathopen{}\left(8r-3x\right)\newline \amp =\left(8r-3x\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (64) \cdot (9) = 576.

The number 576 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(576)\amp -1(-576)\amp 2(288)\amp -2(-288)
ewline3(192)\amp -3(-192)\amp 4(144)\amp -4(-144)
ewline6(96)\amp -6(-96)\amp 8(72)\amp -8(-72)
ewline9(64)\amp -9(-64)\amp 12(48)\amp -12(-48)
ewline16(36)\amp -16(-36)\amp 18(32)\amp -18(-32)
ewline24(24)\amp -24(-24)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of -48, and this sum happens with (-24)+(-24)=-48 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]64r^{2}-48rx+9x^{2}\amp =64r^{2}-24rx-24rx+9x^{2}
ewline \amp =8r\mathopen{}\left(8r-3xight)-3x\mathopen{}\left(8r-3xight)
ewline \amp =\left(8r-3xight)\mathopen{}\left(8r-3xight)
ewline \amp =\left(8r-3xight)^{2}\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 64r^{2}-48rx+9x^{2} into the top left and bottom right rectangles:

The first term's coefficient, 64, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(64)\amp 2(32)\amp 4(16)\amp 8(8)\newline\end{array}
The third term's coefficient, 9, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(9)\amp -1(-9)\amp 3(3)\amp -3(-3)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 64r^{2}-48rx+9x^{2} by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:

Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]64r^{2}-48rx+9x^{2}\amp =\left(8r-3x\right)\mathopen{}\left(8r-3x\right)\newline \amp =\left(8r-3x\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
